title: Examining Scaling and Transfer of Language Model Architectures for Machine Translation

structured.summary: The paper investigates the efficacy of language model architectures compared to traditional encoder-decoder models for machine translation, focusing on different scaling behaviors and cross-lingual transfer capabilities. It examines architectural choices affecting LM performance in bilingual, multilingual, and zero-shot translation tasks, showing LMs can match EncDec performance in certain contexts but lag in computational efficiency.

strengths.evidence: The study thoroughly examines various configurations of LM architectures compared to the traditional EncDec structure and explores multiple design choices.
It provides insights into how scaling behavior varies between LM and EncDec models, with effects diminishing as model size increases.
The study highlights that PrefixLM LMs excel in zero-shot settings by significantly reducing off-target translations.

weaknesses.evidence: The paper primarily analyzes existing LM architectures without introducing new model forms or substantial innovations over prior works like those by He et al. (2018) and Wang et al. (2021).
The paper lacks specifics on datasets used, hyperparameter selection, optimization strategies, and does not provide supplementary material for replication.
The paper predominantly assesses translation quality without thorough analysis of robustness, efficiency trade-offs, or performance under domain shifts.

questions.question: How can the findings related to zero-shot translation be further validated with new datasets or settings?
questions.motivation: To assess the generalizability and real-world applicability of the potential improvements in zero-shot translation.

critique_points.point_001: Limited novelty.. The paper primarily analyzes existing LM architectures without introducing new model forms or substantial innovations over prior works like those by He et al. (2018) and Wang et al. (2021)
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient methodological detail for reproducibility.. The paper lacks specifics on datasets used, hyperparameter selection, optimization strategies, and does not provide supplementary material for replication
critique_points.point_003: Lack of robust evaluation.. The paper predominantly assesses translation quality without thorough analysis of robustness, efficiency trade-offs, or performance under domain shifts
